$SPCX Starlink Wi-Fi is now live on United Airlines international flights.

United is accelerating the rollout and expects nearly 1,000 Starlink-equipped aircraft by year-end.

$MU and Anthropic announced a strategic AI agreement covering memory, storage, Claude adoption, and a Micron investment in Anthropic's Series H.

The deal positions Micron as both a key memory supplier for AI infrastructure and a direct partner to one of the leading AI labs.

$DELL unveiled its PowerEdge XE8812 AI server built on $NVDA Vera Rubin NVL144 architecture for large-scale AI and HPC workloads.

The system scales to 144 GPUs per rack with liquid cooling and reinforces how the next AI infrastructure cycle is already forming around Vera Rubin.

$WYFI is up over 17% today and remains one of the more interesting smaller AI infrastructure names I've been researching and shared a deep dive with the community because it sits in the same broad theme as neocloud and AI data center companies.

WhiteFiber has two core businesses through cloud services that give customers access to GPU-powered high-performance compute and colocation services that provide data center space, power, cooling and infrastructure for customers running their own equipment.

The biggest catalyst I'm watching is NC-1 which could really change the revenue base if Phase 1, Phase 2 and later expansion capacity come online as planned.

$MSFT CEO Satya Nadella warned that the AI race cannot become a world where a few companies control the learning, labor disruption & power needed to build data centers.

Microsoft is pushing cheaper models & Copilot agents to lower costs & reduce dependence on frontier labs.

$PLTR is expected to start generating ~$10B in annual operating income by 2028.

Palantir sits at most defensible layer of the AI stack, with LLMs as one input, systems of record as another and the ontology serving as the operating layer that turns intelligence into decisions.

$SPCX signed a compute deal with open-source AI startup Reflection AI for access to $NVDA GB300 chips at Colossus 2.

Reflection will pay SpaceX $150M per month starting July 1 totaling about $6.3B if the deal runs through 2029.
$GOOGL DOWN OVER 6% TODAY  😳
$SPCX DOWN MORE THAN 10% TODAY

$GOOGL is investing ~$75M in A24 marking its first film studio stake through a multi-year AI research partnership with Google DeepMind.

A24 and DeepMind plan to build AI tools for film production and distribution.
$SPCX NOW HAS OVER $100B IN CASH

$META is investing $900M in Indian fintech CRED for ~20% stake as it pushes to turn WhatsApp into a larger payments and commerce platform.

CRED founder Kunal Shah will join Meta full time as head of WhatsApp bringing proven payments experience in WhatsApp’s largest market.

$NVDA launched Halos for Robotics which is a full-stack safety system for robotics and physical AI.

The platform gives robot developers one safety architecture across compute, sensors, software, external monitoring and AI certification.

This is exactly why $MU has such a unique position in the AI economy. 

It's the only American HBM maker with government backing so in a world that cares where chips come from and with HBM the scarcest asset in the entire buildout right now, it has a ton of pricing power.

📺 WHY THE CHARTS DON'T SUPPORT A BEARISH THESIS YET + $MU AHEAD OF EARNINGS, $INTC SETUP, $SPCX UPDATE

Don't miss today's newsletter: t3live.com/rd

Last week was a great reminder that Fed Day is not always the final answer. After Kevin Warsh delivered what many interpreted as a hawkish message, traders quickly turned bearish as markets sold off into Wednesday’s close. But by the end of Thursday, the market did what it often does: it forced participants to rethink their assumptions.

The key takeaway I want you to learn is that the charts still do not support a bearish thesis.

*

$SPY peaked near $760 on June 1 and has spent the last three weeks consolidating rather than correcting. 

Instead of giving back gains through a sharp decline, the market has worked off overbought conditions over time. 

I see an inside wedge pattern forming, with $SPY $743 acting as the critical level. As long as $SPY remains above $743, it is difficult to become aggressively bearish. 

Resistance sits near $748, and the market remains trapped between support and resistance while deciding its next move.

*

Technology continues to be the strongest area of the market. The $QQQ have held up far better than many expected following the Fed reaction, closing above Wednesday’s high and maintaining a constructive technical posture. 

Important support remains near $732, while the broader trend remains intact.

*

Leadership stocks are sending an even stronger message. 

Micron $MU, for example, continues to trade at or near all-time highs heading into earnings on June 24. The stock remains above its 8-day and continues to attract buyers. 

Why would anyone choose to short a stock making new highs while major indexes remain healthy? Remember, strong stocks tend to stay strong until proven otherwise.

*

Intel $INTC is another name showing constructive behavior. 

Rather than breaking down, it has spent roughly six weeks digesting prior gains and building a base. That consolidation is now beginning to resolve higher, giving bulls a potential continuation setup.

I have some $INTC still.

*

We had a fantastic $SPCX IPO trade last week.

My focus now is on whether $SPCX can hold the $172 area and begin rebuilding after its initial price discovery phase. 

Other space-related names, such as $ASTS and $RKLB, continue to struggle. No capital move from $SPCX to them, yet.

*

So, my broader message today is about process over prediction. 

Markets remain in a healthy consolidation after a powerful rally, semiconductors continue to look strong, and many leadership stocks are making new highs. 

Until key support levels break, the evidence points to consolidation rather than correction. For now, the bulls still have the benefit of the doubt.

*

Global investor attention on AI semiconductors has largely centered on the US, South Korea, and Taiwan.

A quieter innovation cycle has been unfolding on China's STAR Market, and three major IPOs are preparing to change that.

@UnitreeRobotics, the global leader in humanoid robotics with the highest humanoid shipment volume worldwide.

ChangXin Memory Technologies, China's leading DRAM manufacturer.

Yangtze Memory Technologies, the world's fourth-largest NAND producer, has licensed its technology to Samsung.

Together, they represent China's move up the AI hardware value chain, from memory and storage to physical AI.

These companies are not yet publicly traded, but $KSTR, our China technology ETF, is designed to potentially capture this innovation.

Read our latest article: bit.ly/4oyRhox

For KSTR's top ten holdings: bit.ly/3SO5L7U

If Bitcoin is a store of value, than everything except money is a store of value (where store of value just means an asset intended to appreciated faster than money depreciates, but involves risk). By the strict meaning of the term, though, nothing is a store of value.
There is zero mention of store of value in the original bitcoin whitepaper. It came later as a market driven narrative that held for quite some time. I
He’s not anti btc- he’s anti stupid narrative
I miss him.
If a store of value goes through cycles, that means it’s not storing value properly in those cycles, WHICH MEANS IT ISN’T A STORE OF VALUE GOODNIGHT
